entries.prompt:
GBT field service team performing on-site rubber lining inside an

Two technicians work inside the vessel applying rubber lining.
FIELD CAPABILITY — not just workshop lining, but on-site service
anywhere in the world.

entries.caption: QC / Holiday Testing
entries.prompt:
Quality control testing at GBT — a QC inspector performing high-
voltage holiday detection on a coated surface. The instrument,
test voltage, and systematic grid pattern are visible. The coated

documented results. GERMAN QC RIGOUR.

entries.caption: Card Thumbnail (400×250)
entries.prompt:
Three-quarter view of a completed FGD absorber tower — a massive
cylindrical vessel (12m diameter × 30m tall) in primer grey,
sitting on transport cradles in the dispatch yard. The tower dwarfs
the workers and vehicles around it. Nozzle connections, access
platforms, and lifting lugs visible. The sheer physical presence
of the absorber communicates the SCALE of Jasmino's capability.
Late afternoon light, industrial yard setting.

entries.caption: Hero (1200×675)
entries.prompt:
Full hero shot of the FGD absorber tower — the same vessel from
the card but photographed from a lower angle to emphasize the
height. The tower fills the frame vertically, with workers at the
base providing scale. The fabrication hall behind provides context.
Sky visible above — the tower reaches toward it. MONUMENTAL
INDUSTRIAL ENGINEERING.

entries.caption: Gallery 1: During Fabrication (600×400)
entries.prompt:
The FGD absorber during fabrication — the tower shell sections
assembled horizontally on welding rotators. The individual shell
courses (each 3m wide, 40mm wall thickness) are being joined by
circumferential welding. Workers on scaffold platforms at multiple
levels. The horizontal assembly method visible — the tower is
built lying down, then tilted vertical for final assembly and
testing. CONSTRUCTION IN PROGRESS.

entries.caption: Gallery 2: Internal Rubber Lining (600×400)
entries.prompt:
Interior of the FGD absorber tower during rubber lining application
— looking upward from the sump, showing the curved walls being
lined with CSM (chlorosulfonated polyethylene) rubber. The lining
is partially complete: the lower sections show finished black
rubber, the upper sections show bare prepared steel awaiting
application. Workers on internal platforms apply the lining
methodically. The interior volume is immense — cathedral-like.
THE CORE VALUE — rubber lining at the largest scale.

entries.caption: Gallery 3: Ready for Dispatch (600×400)
entries.prompt:
The completed FGD absorber loaded on a multi-axle hydraulic
modular transporter (SPMT) for heavy transport. The vessel is
secured with heavy chains and cradles. The transport trailer
has 12+ axle lines — each wheel visible. Police escort vehicles
and route survey equipment staged nearby. The vessel leaves the
facility on a journey that requires power line raising, bridge
load assessments, and traffic management. DELIVERY — the final
step, and an engineering challenge in itself.

entries.caption: Card Thumbnail (400×250)
entries.prompt:
Overview of a phosphoric acid reactor battery — multiple large
agitated reactors (4m diameter each) arranged in series, connected
by overflow piping. The reactors have top-mounted agitator drives
and are rubber-lined internally. The battery sits on a concrete
plinth in a chemical plant setting. CHEMICAL PROCESSING at
production scale.

entries.caption: Hero (1200×675)
entries.prompt:
Full-frame hero of the reactor battery — perspective shot along
the line of reactors, showing all units in the cascade. The lead
reactor is closest to camera, with the others receding into depth.
Each reactor's agitator drive, instrumentation, and piping
connections visible. The plant setting provides industrial context.
SYSTEMATIC DESIGN — multiple identical vessels for continuous
production.

entries.caption: Gallery 1: Fabrication (600×400)
entries.prompt:
A phosphoric acid reactor vessel during fabrication — the 4m
diameter shell with internal baffles and agitator bearing housing
being welded. The vessel is oriented vertically in the fab hall,
with workers on platforms. Nozzle connections (feed, overflow,
drain, instrument) at specific elevations. CUSTOM FABRICATION.

entries.caption: Gallery 2: EPDM Rubber Lining (600×400)
entries.prompt:
EPDM rubber lining being applied inside the reactor — the
distinctive grey-black EPDM rubber sheets being bonded to the
prepared steel substrate. A technician works on the complex
geometry around a baffle-to-wall junction — cutting, fitting,
and bonding the rubber to follow the contours exactly. The
EPDM provides chemical resistance to the phosphoric acid
process environment. PRECISION LINING.

entries.caption: Gallery 3: On-Site Installation (600×400)
entries.prompt:
The completed reactor battery installed on-site at the fertilizer
plant. The reactors are connected by process piping, instrumented,
and ready for commissioning. Workers make final piping connections.
The plant infrastructure visible: pipe racks, cable trays, access
platforms. A real project, delivered and installed. PROVEN DELIVERY.

entries.caption: Card Thumbnail (400×250)
entries.prompt:
Overview of a desalination pre-treatment system — FRP filtration
vessels in a battery, chemical dosing skids, and process piping
arranged on a concrete plinth. The system is compact and modular.
The FRP vessels are distinctive cream-white. WATER TREATMENT
engineering.

entries.caption: Hero (1200×675)
entries.prompt:
Full hero of the pre-treatment system — perspective view showing
the complete system from intake to outlet. FRP vessels, pipe
interconnections, instrumentation and control panel visible. The
system is operational — water flowing, pumps running. Coastal
or arid setting appropriate for desalination. COMPLETE SYSTEM
delivery.

entries.caption: Gallery 1: FRP Vessels in Workshop (600×400)
entries.prompt:
FRP filtration vessels being fabricated in the Jasmino composite

The vessels show the hand-layup and filament winding construction.
Nozzle flanges being attached. The workshop scale — multiple
vessels simultaneously — shows PRODUCTION CAPACITY.

entries.caption: Gallery 2: Dosing Skid Assembly (600×400)
entries.prompt:
Chemical dosing skid being assembled — a structural steel frame
supporting metering pumps, small storage tanks, piping with

wire the control panel and connect instrument cables. A complete,
pre-assembled module ready for factory testing. MODULAR
ENGINEERING.

entries.caption: Gallery 3: Site Installation (600×400)
entries.prompt:
The desalination pre-treatment system being installed on site —

connect piping between modules. The coastal or arid setting
establishes the application. SITE DELIVERY AND INSTALLATION.

entries.caption: Card Thumbnail (400×250)
entries.prompt:
Brine purification vessels — a series of rubber-lined or plastic-
lined steel vessels for chlor-alkali brine treatment. The vessels

addition, sampling, and process flow. Chemical plant setting.
CHEMICAL RESISTANT EQUIPMENT.

entries.caption: Hero (1200×675)
entries.prompt:
Full hero of the chlor-alkali brine system installed at a Middle

handles aggressive brine chemistry — PVDF and rubber linings
protecting every wetted surface. INTERNATIONAL DELIVERY.

entries.caption: Gallery 1: Fabrication (600×400)
entries.prompt:
Brine purification vessels during fabrication — multiple vessels
at different stages on the fab hall floor. Shell assembly, nozzle
welding, internal inspection visible across the batch. The batch
nature of the order — 6+ vessels of similar design — shows
PRODUCTION EFFICIENCY.

entries.caption: Gallery 2: PVDF Lining (600×400)
entries.prompt:
PVDF lining application inside a brine vessel — the bright white
thermoplastic sheets being welded in place. The contrast of white
PVDF against dark steel. A technician uses a hot-air welding gun
on a seam. The PVDF provides the chemical resistance required for
chlor-alkali electrolysis brine. PLASTIC LINING EXPERTISE.

entries.caption: Gallery 3: Completed Installation (600×400)
entries.prompt:
The completed brine purification battery installed at the chlor-
alkali plant — vessels connected, instrumented, and in service.
Process flows visible, operators monitoring from a local panel.
The plant is in a Middle East setting — appropriate architecture
and landscape. OPERATING IN THE FIELD.

entries.caption: Card Thumbnail (400×250)

Overview of copper leaching vessels — large open-top rubber-lined

Semi-arid mining landscape. MINING AND HYDROMETALLURGY.

entries.caption: Hero (1200×675)
entries.prompt:
Full hero of the leaching vessel array — aerial or elevated view
showing the full cascade of tanks, overflow connections, and the
leach pad in the background. The mining operation context: earth-

The blue-green leach solution in the open-top tanks. INDUSTRIAL
MINING at scale.

entries.caption: Gallery 1: Vessel Fabrication (600×400)
entries.prompt:
A leach vessel during fabrication — large open-top tank (6m

HEAVY TANK FABRICATION.

entries.caption: Gallery 2: Dual-Compound Rubber Lining (600×400)
entries.prompt:
Dual-compound rubber lining inside a leach vessel — the lining

bonding layer for adhesion, and a soft synthetic rubber wear
layer for abrasion and chemical resistance. The technician
applies the wear layer over the bonding layer — the two
distinct black/grey rubber layers visible at the seam edge.
ENGINEERED LINING SYSTEMS — multiple rubber types for
optimized performance.

entries.caption: Gallery 3: Rubber-Lined Piping (600×400)
entries.prompt:
Rubber-lined pipe sections being installed — large-bore (600mm+)
steel pipes with internal rubber lining visible at the flanged
connections. The lining extends through the pipe bore and wraps
over the flange face as a gasket. Pipe sections being bolted
together in the field by installation technicians. COMPLETE
SYSTEM PROTECTION — not just vessels, but the entire piping
circuit is lined.

entries.caption: Card Thumbnail (400×250)
entries.prompt:
A tall distillation column at a refinery/chemical plant — the
column is 3m diameter × 25m tall, with external platforms,
piping connections, and instrumentation. The column is undergoing
a turnaround revamp — scaffolding erected around it, manways
open, maintenance activities visible. PLANT MAINTENANCE.

entries.caption: Hero (1200×675)
entries.prompt:
Full hero of the distillation column revamp — the column in the
plant context, surrounded by scaffolding and maintenance equipment.
Workers at various levels. The column's height dominates the frame.
Adjacent equipment (reboiler, condenser, reflux drum) visible.
The plant is shut down for the turnaround — a controlled, planned
maintenance campaign. TURNAROUND ENGINEERING.

entries.caption: Gallery 1: Column During Turnaround (600×400)
entries.prompt:
Inside the column during turnaround — looking upward through the
tray stack. Old trays removed, column shell visible with surface
damage from years of service (corrosion, erosion patterns). Workers
on internal platforms inspect the shell condition. Removed tray
sections stacked nearby for disposal. THE PROBLEM — what time and
chemistry do to unprotected steel.

entries.caption: Gallery 2: New Tray Installation (600×400)
entries.prompt:
New duplex stainless steel trays being installed inside the column
— precision-machined tray panels, downcomer assemblies, and support
rings. A technician positions a tray section using a lifting davit
rigged at the column top. The new trays are bright, clean duplex
stainless — significantly more corrosion-resistant than the
originals. THE SOLUTION — upgraded materials for extended life.

entries.caption: Gallery 3: On-Site Rubber Lining (600×400)
entries.prompt:
The HAW field service team performing on-site rubber lining of the
column interior — sections between tray support rings are being
lined with rubber to protect the carbon steel shell from corrosion.
The team works in confined space conditions: harnesses, ventilation,
portable lighting. The rubber lining is applied to the prepared
steel surface between the new tray supports. FIELD LINING — HAW's
specialty, performed anywhere in the world.

entries.caption: FGD Absorber Delivered

A heavy transport convoy — the massive FGD absorber tower loaded

DELIVERY EVENT.

A trade show exhibition stand — the Jasmino booth at ACHEMA

Visitors browse the stand (shown from behind). The exhibition

INDUSTRY PRESENCE.

Vessel loading at a port facility — rubber-lined brine vessels

A conference presentation setting — a speaker at a podium with a

entries.caption: Phosphoric Acid Storage Farm
entries.prompt:
A completed phosphoric acid storage farm — 8 large rubber-lined

Site personnel and Jasmino commissioning engineers together (shown

A European certification context — the PED (Pressure Equipment

entries.caption: Our Story (`/videos/ourstory.mp4`)
entries.prompt:
Slow tracking shot through the fabrication hall — camera moves
at walking pace through the main bay, passing between enormous
vessels under construction. Workers visible at their stations.
The camera movement reveals the depth and scale of the facility
progressively — each new vessel and work zone appears as the
camera advances. Historical feel — the company's FIVE DECADES of
manufacturing embodied in the physical space. Warm tungsten task
lights, cool overhead ambient, documentary camera movement.

entries.caption: Jasmino Group (`/videos/jasmino-group.mp4`)
entries.prompt:
Montage sequence cutting between three facilities: Jasmino India
(heavy fabrication), HAW Germany (rubber lining), GBT Germany
(coatings). Each facility gets 3 seconds — establishing shot
showing the scale and character of each location. India: steel,
welding, cranes. HAW: rubber, autoclaves, precision. GBT:
coatings, spray bays, German orderliness. The cuts are smooth,
tempo is measured, GLOBAL CAPABILITY conveyed through three
distinct industrial environments.

entries.caption: Infrastructure (`/videos/infra.mp4`)
entries.prompt:
Aerial slow track over the Jasmino India campus — camera rises
from ground level (showing the fabrication hall entrance with a
vessel emerging on transport) to 60 meters altitude, revealing
the full campus layout. The campus fills the frame — 80,000 m²
of production capability. The aerial perspective emphasizes
CAMPUS SCALE. Golden hour light, Maharashtra industrial landscape.

entries.caption: Engineering Design T2 (`/videos/eng-design-t2.mp4`)
entries.prompt:
Slow camera move along a row of engineering workstations — monitors
displaying 3D plant models, FEA results, P&IDs. The camera glides
past each station, the screen content changing. Engineers' hands
on keyboards (no faces). The screen glow illuminates the
workstations in the otherwise softly-lit office. DIGITAL
ENGINEERING — the quiet intensity of design work. Cool blue
screen glow, rhythmic camera movement.

entries.caption: Equipment Manufacturing T2 (`/videos/manu-t2.mp4`)
entries.prompt:
Montage of fabrication processes: plate rolling (steel bending
through massive rolls), welding (SAW arc visible, sparks), crane
operations (a dished head being lowered onto a vessel shell), NDT
(radiographic testing setup). Each process gets 2–3 seconds. The
scale of equipment and the precision of operations. MANUFACTURING
POWER. Warm tungsten and welding glow, industrial sound implied
by visual intensity.

entries.caption: Corrosion Protection T2 (`/videos/corrosion-t2.mp4`)
entries.prompt:
The lining process sequence: blast cleaning (abrasive hitting
steel, creating white-metal finish) → primer application (brush
coating on prepared surface) → rubber sheet cutting (patterns
being cut on a table) → application (technician pressing rubber
inside vessel) → autoclave (vessel entering the autoclave) →
spark testing (holiday detector sweeping the finished lining).
PROCESS SEQUENCE — the complete lining workflow in 10 seconds.
Each step transitions smoothly to the next.

entries.caption: Rubber Products T2 (`/videos/rubber-t2.mp4`)
entries.prompt:
Compound development to finished product: lab (rheometer testing)
→ mixing (Banbury mixer operating) → sheeting (two-roll mill) →
moulding (press opening to reveal finished parts) → testing
(tensile tester pulling a specimen) → products (display of
finished rubber parts). THE JOURNEY from chemistry to component.
Each step gets 1.5–2 seconds. Lab white to factory warm to
product display.
